title: System and method for producing magnetite from red mud using two stage reactors
abstract: Disclosed are systems and methods to produce magnetite from red mud using two-stage reactors (102-N). Each two-stage reactor (102-N) includes a lower section (104) and an upper section (106), which includes an electric furnace (110). The reactor (102-N) includes a first stage (112) and a second stage (114) for processing red mud and coal particles separately. The reactors (102-N) are configured to operate in a charging mode, pre-heating mode, reaction mode, and discharging mode sequentially with a predetermined time lag. The reactors (102-N) are connected serially to allow transfer of pre-heated air, which improves energy efficiency during the magnetite production.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A system (100) for continuous extraction of magnetite from red mud, the system comprising: a plurality of two-stage reactors (102-N) attached in a serial connection, each two- stage reactor (102) comprising: a lower section (104) comprising an inlet (108) to receive input gas and an upper section (106) comprising an electric furnace (110) with thermal insulation, forming a first stage (112) and a second stage (114), wherein each two-stage reactor (102-N) is configured to operate in: a charging mode to charge the first stage (112) with coal particles and the second stage (114) with red mud particles, wherein the first stage (112) is heated to a first temperature and the second stage (114) to a second predetermined temperature; a pre-heating mode to receive, at the first stage (112), a supply of hot gas from the lower section (104); a first reaction mode to produce, in the first stage, mixture of reducing gases by gasification of coal; a second reaction mode to produce magnetite, in the second stage (114), by fluidization of the red mud particles and reduction of hematite using the mixture of reducing gases; and a discharging mode to discharge the magnetite into a slurry tank (120) having water therein to form a slurry thereof, wherein, each two-stage reactor (102-N) operates in charging mode, pre-heating mode, first and second reaction modes, and discharging mode sequentially with a predetermined time lag At. 2. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, further comprising a wet magnetic separator (124) configured to separate magnetite from the non-magnetic particles in the slurry. 3. The system as claimed in claim 1, comprising 4 or more two-stage reactors (102-N), wherein the reactors are configured to operate in charging, preheating, reaction and discharging modes with a time lag At, and wherein the each two-stage reactor (102- N) operating in the reaction modes produces hot gas, that is supplied to another two- stage reactor (102-N) operating in pre-heating mode, and each two-stage reactor (102-N) operating in the discharging mode receives cold gas that is heated and supplied as hot input gas to another two-stage reactor operating in pre-heating mode. 4. The system as claimed in claim 1, comprising a control unit configured to control gas flow and heating of the electric furnace in each of the reactors to ensure that the second predetermined temperature is in the range 700-800 Â°C and to divert hot or cold gas to the reactors in sequence with the predetermined time lag At. 5. A method of producing magnetite from red mud using a plurality of two- stage reactors (102-N) attached in serial connection, the method comprising: charging coal particles in a first stage (112) and red mud fines comprising hematite in a second stage (114) of each two stage reactor (102) sequentially with a predetermined time lag At; heating the first stage (112) to a first temperature and the second stage (114) to a second predetermined temperature in each two-stage reactor (102) with the predetermined time lag At; receiving input gas for gasification of the coal particles; producing a mixture of reducing gases in the first stage in each two-stage reactor (102) with the predetermined time lag At; fluidizing the red mud particles and reducing the hematite using the mixture of reducing gases in the second stage (114) to produce magnetite in each of the two-stage reactors (102) with the predetermined time lag At; and 

 discharging the magnetite from each two-stage reactor (102-N) into a slurry tank (120) containing water with the predetermined time-lag At to obtain a magnetite slurry. 6. The method as claimed in claim 5, wherein the magnetite slurry is processed using magnetic separation to obtain magnetite of concentration 95% or better. 7. The method as claimed in claim 5, further comprising supplying hot gas during the magnetite production in a first two-stage reactor (102-N) to a second two- stage reactor (102-N) as input gas. 8. The method as claimed in claim 5, comprising supplying cold gases to a two-stage reactor (102) before discharge of the magnetite, wherein the cold gases are heated and circulated to another two-stage reactor (102) as input gas. 9. The method as claimed in claim 5, wherein the second predetermined temperature is in the range 700-800Â°C. 10. The method as claimed in claim 5, wherein the coal is low grade coal of particle size 4-8 mm and the red mud is of particle size between 50-90 pm. 11. A two-stage reactor apparatus (102) for producing magnetite from red mud, the apparatus comprising: a lower section (104) comprising an inlet (108) to receive input gas; an upper section (106) comprising an electric furnace (110) with thermal insulation, the electric furnace comprising a first stage (112) loaded with coal particles and a second stage (114) loaded with red mud particles, wherein the apparatus is configured to: 

 heat the first stage (112) to a first temperature to gasify the coal and the second stage (114) to a second predetermined temperature; receive, at the first stage (112), hot gas from the lower section (104) to produce a mixture of reducing gases obtained from gasification of the coal; and produce, at the second stage (114), magnetite by fluidization of the red mud particles and reduction of hematite using the mixture of reducing gases. 12. The apparatus as claimed in claim 11, comprising a first perforated gas distributor plate (116) separating the lower section and the upper section, and a second perforated gas distributor plate (118) separating the first stage (112) and the second stage (114). 13. The apparatus as claimed in claim 11, comprising a thermocouple (122) configured to measure temperature of the red mud particles in the second stage (114) and a rotameter at the inlet to measure gas flow rate. 14. The apparatus as claimed in claim 13, comprising a control unit configured to control gas flow and heating of the electric furnace to ensure that the second predetermined temperature is in the range 700-800 Â°C.
